title: The Database Index You're Missing Is the One Killing Your Production
description: How to stop guessing and start reading query plans. A field guide to indexing strategies that engineers avoid looking at because the truth is uncomfortable.

title: Distributed Tracing: Or How I Learned to Stop Guessing and Read the Spans
description: If your debugging strategy is reading logs in five different tabs while screaming into the void, distributed tracing is for you. A practical guide to OpenTelemetry.

title: Understanding Go Routines and Channels
description: Why Go's concurrency model (CSP) is superior to thread-per-request systems and how to avoid channel deadlocks.

title: JVM Tuning for Low-Latency Systems
description: A deep dive into Garbage Collection tuning, CPU pinning, and memory alignment for high-frequency trading applications.

title: Kafka Is Not a Database (And Other Things I've Had to Say Out Loud)
description: Apache Kafka is a log. A brilliant, battle-tested, infinitely scalable log. It is not a query engine, it is not a database, and it should not be your primary source of truth. Let's discuss.

title: Kubernetes Is Not a Silver Bullet (It's More Like a Swiss Army Chainsaw)
description: K8s solves real problems. It also introduces approximately 47 new ones. A guide to knowing what you're getting into before you commit the entire org.

title: Load Testing Is Not Optional: A Guide to Not Discovering Limits in Production
description: Your service has a breaking point. You will find it. The only question is whether it's during a controlled test or during peak traffic on a launch day. Choose wisely.

title: Microservices: The Good Parts, The Bad Parts, and the Parts That Will End Your On-Call Rotation
description: Microservices solve the problem of large monoliths being hard to scale and deploy. They introduce the problem of distributed systems being hard to understand and debug. A balanced view from the trenches.

title: The On-Call Handbook Nobody Gave You
description: A practical survival guide for engineers entering on-call rotation for the first time, from someone who has been paged during dinner, during sleep, and once during a wedding.

title: Redis Patterns That Don't Age Like Milk
description: Cache invalidation is famously hard. Redis is famously fast. This post explains how to use Redis correctly so you don't end up serving stale data with confidence.

title: API Design Is Communication. Stop Making Your APIs Scream.
description: A field guide to REST API design decisions that prevent your consumers from filing bug reports that are actually just documentation complaints.

title: Technical Features (Markdown renderer testing)
description: A test post for LaTeX rendering and Mermaid diagrams.
